---
title: "Predictive Inventory Optimisation Dashboard"
description: "An applied consultancy project with Agvance Ltd & Animal Farmacy - building an ML-powered demand forecasting dashboard to eliminate stock-outs for an agri-nutrition SME, integrating with live Shopify, Turbo and MINTsoft systems."
author: "Ruairí Cullen, Alex Meehan, Woo-Sung Hollenhorst et al"
date: "2026-01-26"
image: "../../images/inventory.png"
categories: [Machine Learning, Demand Forecasting, Consultancy, Python, CRISP-DM]
toc: true
toc-location: left
---

## Overview

::: {.callout-note}
🔄 **Status: In Progress** - Research Phase (Phase 2 of 4), January 2026 - August 2026.
:::

This is a live Applied Consultancy Project at DCU, working with a 6-person team for 
**Agvance Ltd** and **Animal Farmacy** - a family-owned agri-nutrition wholesaler and 
e-commerce retailer based in Portlaoise, Co. Laois.

The client manages over 700 products across a shared warehouse, supplying farms across 
Ireland and the UK. Despite consistent year-on-year growth, recurring **stock-outs** and 
an experience-driven ordering process are creating operational inefficiencies and lost revenue.

---

## The Business Problem

::: {.callout-warning appearance="minimal"}
Agvance and Animal Farmacy currently rely on experience-based manual reordering, resulting 
in regular stock imbalances, avoidable stock-outs, and inefficient working capital 
allocation — particularly during seasonal demand peaks.
:::

| Challenge | Impact |
|---|---|
| Experience-driven ordering | Unpredictable stock-outs |
| No demand forecasting system | Missed sales, customer dissatisfaction |
| Seasonal demand patterns | Peaks unaccounted for in reorder triggers |
| Multi-system data (Shopify, Turbo, MINTsoft) | No unified data view for decisions |

---

## Data Sources

Two real client datasets were used for the EDA phase:

| Source | Period | Records |
|---|---|---|
| Turbo Inventory CSV | Jan 2022 – Mar 2025 | 33,213 rows |
| Shopify Daily Orders ODS | Jan 2025 – Apr 2026 | 9,700 rows |

---

## Python Code - EDA Setup & Data Loading

```{python}
#| code-fold: true
#| code-summary: "Show imports and styling"

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.ticker as mticker
import warnings
warnings.filterwarnings("ignore")

# Colour theme matching client branding
TURBO_COLOR   = "#2196F3"
SHOPIFY_COLOR = "#4CAF50"
ACCENT        = "#FF5722"

plt.rcParams.update({
    "figure.dpi": 130,
    "axes.titlesize": 13,
    "axes.labelsize": 11,
    "figure.autolayout": True
})
print("Libraries loaded.")
```

## Python Code - Simulated Monthly Revenue Trend

::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal"}
The charts below use simulated data that mirrors the structure and patterns of the 
real client dataset. Actual figures are commercially sensitive.
:::

```{python}
#| fig-cap: "Monthly revenue trend across Turbo and Shopify channels (simulated)"
#| echo: true

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.ticker as mticker

np.random.seed(42)

# Simulate Turbo monthly revenue (Jan 2022 – Mar 2025)
turbo_periods = pd.date_range("2022-01", periods=39, freq="MS")
turbo_base    = 45000
turbo_season  = 12000 * np.sin(2 * np.pi * (np.arange(39) - 2) / 12)
turbo_trend   = np.linspace(0, 15000, 39)
turbo_rev     = turbo_base + turbo_season + turbo_trend + np.random.normal(0, 3000, 39)

# Simulate Shopify monthly revenue (Jan 2025 – Apr 2026)
shop_periods  = pd.date_range("2025-01", periods=16, freq="MS")
shop_base     = 18000
shop_season   = 5000 * np.sin(2 * np.pi * (np.arange(16) - 2) / 12)
shop_rev      = shop_base + shop_season + np.random.normal(0, 1500, 16)

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(13, 5))
ax.plot(turbo_periods, turbo_rev, color="#2196F3",
        linewidth=2, label="Turbo (2022–2025)")
ax.fill_between(turbo_periods, turbo_rev, alpha=0.1, color="#2196F3")
ax.plot(shop_periods, shop_rev, color="#4CAF50",
        linewidth=2, label="Shopify (2025–2026)")
ax.fill_between(shop_periods, shop_rev, alpha=0.1, color="#4CAF50")
ax.yaxis.set_major_formatter(
    mticker.FuncFormatter(lambda x, _: f"€{x:,.0f}"))
ax.set_title("Monthly Revenue Trend — Turbo vs Shopify (Simulated)",
             fontweight="bold")
ax.set_xlabel("Month")
ax.set_ylabel("Revenue (€)")
ax.legend(fontsize=10)
plt.xticks(rotation=45)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

## Python Code - Seasonal Revenue Heatmap

```{python}
#| fig-cap: "Seasonal revenue heatmap showing monthly demand patterns by year (simulated)"
#| echo: true

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np

# Simulate seasonal heatmap data (2022–2024)
month_labels = ["Jan","Feb","Mar","Apr","May","Jun",
                "Jul","Aug","Sep","Oct","Nov","Dec"]
years = [2022, 2023, 2024]

np.random.seed(7)
data = np.array([
    [38000 + 8000*np.sin(np.pi*(m-2)/6) + np.random.normal(0, 2000)
     for m in range(12)]
    for _ in years
])

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(13, 4))
im = ax.imshow(data, cmap="Blues", aspect="auto")
ax.set_xticks(range(12))
ax.set_xticklabels(month_labels)
ax.set_yticks(range(len(years)))
ax.set_yticklabels(years)
ax.set_title("Seasonal Revenue Heatmap — Turbo Inventory (Simulated)",
             fontweight="bold", pad=12)

for i in range(len(years)):
    for j in range(12):
        ax.text(j, i, f"€{data[i,j]/1000:.0f}k",
                ha="center", va="center", fontsize=7,
                color="white" if data[i,j] > 50000 else "black")

plt.colorbar(im, ax=ax, label="Revenue (€)")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

---

## Methodology

### Qualitative Strand
- **Semi-structured interviews** with CEO, Head of Warehouse Operations, and industry experts
- **On-site observation** and business process mapping of current reorder workflow
- **Thematic analysis** to identify operational requirements and usability needs
- **PRISMA systematic literature review** of state-of-the-art inventory management systems

### Quantitative Strand
- **CRISP-DM framework** for structured ML model development
- **Data sources:** Shopify EPOS, Turbo WMS, MINTsoft — SKU-level historical sales
- **Models under evaluation:** ARIMA, Prophet, XGBoost, LSTM
- **Evaluation metrics:** MAE, RMSE, MSE across identical train/test splits

---

```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'theme': 'base', 'themeVariables': {'edgeLabelBackground':'#ffffff', 'lineColor':'#1a3a2a', 'arrowheadColor':'#1a3a2a'}}}%%
flowchart LR
  A[Data Collection] --> B[EDA]
  B --> C[Model Training]
  C --> D[Evaluation]
  D --> E[Dashboard]
```
After model training, forecast accuracy will be evaluated using Mean Absolute Error:
$$MAE = \frac{1}{n} \sum_{i=1}^{n} |y_i - \hat{y}_i|$$

---

## Current Progress

- [x] Phase 1 Complete - Client briefing, team formation, project proposal submitted
- [x] PRISMA literature review underway - Google Scholar search strategy defined
- [x] On-site warehouse visit completed - As-Is process maps drafted
- [x] Semi-structured interviews conducted with CEO and warehouse operatives
- [x] EDA completed on Turbo (33,213 rows) and Shopify (9,700 rows) datasets
- [ ] Quantitative model training and comparison (Phase 3 - commencing soon)
- [ ] Dashboard development and client integration
- [ ] Final presentation to client and DCU

---

## Project Overview & Details

::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal"}
- **Client:** Agvance Ltd & Animal Farmacy, Portlaoise, Co. Laois
- **Team:** Ruairí Cullen, Alexander Meehan, Woo-Sung Hollenhorst, Gleb Kurilenko, Raunit Budhiraja, Avinash Reddy Yakkanti
- **Supervisor:** Prof. Pierangelo Rosati, DCU Business School
- **Duration:** January 2026 - August 2026
:::

---

## Skills Used

`Python` `Pandas` `Matplotlib` `CRISP-DM` `PRISMA` `Demand Forecasting`
`Business Process Mapping` `Mixed Methods Research` `EDA` `Consultancy`
